zone_6b_notes: Hardy perennial in Zone 6b — spreads aggressively via underground runners and will take over garden beds if not contained. Plant in containers or bury a root barrier 12 inches deep around the planting area. Farmington's moisture and moderate summers suit mint very well. Dies back to ground in winter and returns vigorously in spring. Harvest throughout growing season; cutting back promotes bushy growth and delays flowering. Best flavor before flowering — pinch off flower stalks to extend harvest.

planting.best_planting_time: Plant starts or divisions in spring or fall

water: moderate — 1–2 inches/week; tolerates moist conditions better than most herbs
